options.content:
### How to read this dashboard

Each series is one **portal RPC socket** reported by one local node. A "peer link" between two
Cloud Data Fabric clusters is really a *pair* of sockets - `type="outgoing"` on the cluster that
initiated the connection, `type="incoming"` on the cluster that accepted it - and each side
reports its own TCP-like stats independently. They are **not guaranteed to be symmetric**: one
direction can be healthy while the other stalls, which is exactly the kind of asymmetric failure
this dashboard is built to surface (see the Throughput and Liveness/Staleness rows, which are
always split into incoming/outgoing panels for that reason, independent of the Connection Type
variable below).

**Labels**: `cluster`/`node_id` identify the *local* node reporting the metric (**Cluster**/**Node**
variables); `peer_cluster_uuid`/`remote_node` identify the far side of the link (**Peer
Cluster**/**Peer Node**); `fs_id` identifies the portal (filesystem replication) session
multiplexed over that link (**Filesystem**); `type` is the socket direction (**Connection Type**).
(`channel` is also present on every series but isn't used as a dashboard filter here.) Every
panel's legend is formatted as `cluster nNODE→REMOTE fsFS_ID (type)` so overlapping series stay
identifiable.

Use the variables above to narrow from "every peer link on every cluster" down to one specific
link and direction - each variable filters by whatever you picked in the ones to its left
(Datasource → Cluster → Node → Peer Cluster → Peer Node → Filesystem → Connection Type).

panels.title: 1. Latency & Jitter
panels.description: Round-trip time and its variance for the underlying socket. Rising or noisy values point at a congested or lossy network path between the two clusters.

targets.expr: qfsd_portal_rpc_socket_round_trip_time_us{cluster=~"$cluster", node_id=~"$node_id", peer_cluster_uuid=~"$peer_cluster_uuid", remote_node=~"$remote_node", fs_id=~"$fs_id", type=~"$type"}

panels.title: Round-Trip Time
panels.description: Measured RTT of the portal RPC socket (round_trip_time_us). Compare the incoming vs. outgoing series for the same peer link - a healthy link usually has comparable RTT in both directions.

targets.expr: qfsd_portal_rpc_socket_round_trip_time_variance_us{cluster=~"$cluster", node_id=~"$node_id", peer_cluster_uuid=~"$peer_cluster_uuid", remote_node=~"$remote_node", fs_id=~"$fs_id", type=~"$type"}

panels.title: RTT Variance (Jitter)
panels.description: Smoothed RTT variance (round_trip_time_variance_us), the jitter term used to size the retransmit timeout. A rise here without a matching RTT rise usually means an intermittently congested or flaky path rather than a steady slowdown.

panels.title: 2. Loss & Retransmits
panels.description: Packet-loss indicators: retransmits currently outstanding, the long-run retransmit rate, and the retransmission timeout (RTO) the socket is using.

targets.expr: qfsd_portal_rpc_socket_current_retransmits{cluster=~"$cluster", node_id=~"$node_id", peer_cluster_uuid=~"$peer_cluster_uuid", remote_node=~"$remote_node", fs_id=~"$fs_id", type=~"$type"}

panels.title: In-Flight Retransmits
panels.description: current_retransmits (gauge): retransmits outstanding right now on this socket. Sustained non-zero values indicate an actively lossy path.

targets.expr: rate(qfsd_portal_rpc_socket_total_retransmits_total{cluster=~"$cluster", node_id=~"$node_id", peer_cluster_uuid=~"$peer_cluster_uuid", remote_node=~"$remote_node", fs_id=~"$fs_id", type=~"$type"}[$__rate_interval])

panels.title: Retransmit Rate
panels.description: Rate of increase of total_retransmits_total. Use this for the long-run loss trend; use "In-Flight Retransmits" for what is happening right now.

targets.expr: qfsd_portal_rpc_socket_retransmit_timer_ms{cluster=~"$cluster", node_id=~"$node_id", peer_cluster_uuid=~"$peer_cluster_uuid", remote_node=~"$remote_node", fs_id=~"$fs_id", type=~"$type"}

panels.title: Retransmission Timeout (RTO)
panels.description: retransmit_timer_ms: current RTO. Tracks RTT/jitter under normal conditions; a value pinned high alongside rising retransmits suggests repeated loss events.

panels.title: 3. Congestion Control
panels.description: TCP-style congestion window and slow-start threshold. A window that repeatedly collapses points at a lossy or congested path; see the note on the sentinel threshold value below.

targets.expr: qfsd_portal_rpc_socket_send_congestion_window{cluster=~"$cluster", node_id=~"$node_id", peer_cluster_uuid=~"$peer_cluster_uuid", remote_node=~"$remote_node", fs_id=~"$fs_id", type=~"$type"}

panels.title: Congestion Window
panels.description: send_congestion_window: how much unacknowledged data the sender currently allows in flight. Sawtooth behavior is normal; a window pinned near zero means the connection cannot get out of congestion-avoidance/loss recovery.

targets.expr: qfsd_portal_rpc_socket_send_slow_start_threshold{cluster=~"$cluster", node_id=~"$node_id", peer_cluster_uuid=~"$peer_cluster_uuid", remote_node=~"$remote_node", fs_id=~"$fs_id", type=~"$type"}

panels.title: Slow-Start Threshold
panels.description: send_slow_start_threshold. NOTE: a flat line at 2147483647 (2^31-1) is the "effectively unlimited" sentinel, not a real byte count - it just means no congestion event has constrained this socket yet. Only values well below that are meaningful signal.

panels.title: 4. Queueing & Backpressure
panels.description: Local read/write buffer occupancy for the socket. Growing queues mean the peer is not draining data as fast as it is being produced (or vice versa).

targets.expr: qfsd_portal_rpc_socket_read_queue_size_bytes{cluster=~"$cluster", node_id=~"$node_id", peer_cluster_uuid=~"$peer_cluster_uuid", remote_node=~"$remote_node", fs_id=~"$fs_id", type=~"$type"}

panels.title: Read Queue Size
panels.description: read_queue_size_bytes: bytes received but not yet consumed locally. A steady climb suggests the local side is falling behind draining incoming data.

targets.expr: qfsd_portal_rpc_socket_write_queue_size_bytes{cluster=~"$cluster", node_id=~"$node_id", peer_cluster_uuid=~"$peer_cluster_uuid", remote_node=~"$remote_node", fs_id=~"$fs_id", type=~"$type"}

panels.title: Write Queue Size
panels.description: write_queue_size_bytes: bytes queued locally waiting to be sent/acked. A steady climb suggests the peer (or the path to it) cannot keep up with data being handed to this socket - classic backpressure symptom.

panels.title: 5. Throughput
panels.description: Bytes and segments moved per second, split into incoming vs. outgoing panels so an asymmetric stall (one direction healthy, the other flat) is obvious at a glance rather than hidden inside one busy legend.

targets.expr: rate(qfsd_portal_rpc_socket_bytes_acked_total{cluster=~"$cluster", node_id=~"$node_id", peer_cluster_uuid=~"$peer_cluster_uuid", remote_node=~"$remote_node", fs_id=~"$fs_id", type=~"$type", type="incoming"}[$__rate_interval])

rate(qfsd_portal_rpc_socket_bytes_received_total{cluster=~"$cluster", node_id=~"$node_id", peer_cluster_uuid=~"$peer_cluster_uuid", remote_node=~"$remote_node", fs_id=~"$fs_id", type=~"$type", type="incoming"}[$__rate_interval])

panels.title: Bytes Throughput – Incoming Sockets
panels.description: rate() of bytes_acked_total / bytes_received_total for incoming sockets only. Compare against the other direction's panel - a healthy link is not necessarily symmetric in absolute bytes, but a direction that flatlines to zero while the other is busy is a red flag.

targets.expr: rate(qfsd_portal_rpc_socket_bytes_acked_total{cluster=~"$cluster", node_id=~"$node_id", peer_cluster_uuid=~"$peer_cluster_uuid", remote_node=~"$remote_node", fs_id=~"$fs_id", type=~"$type", type="outgoing"}[$__rate_interval])

rate(qfsd_portal_rpc_socket_bytes_received_total{cluster=~"$cluster", node_id=~"$node_id", peer_cluster_uuid=~"$peer_cluster_uuid", remote_node=~"$remote_node", fs_id=~"$fs_id", type=~"$type", type="outgoing"}[$__rate_interval])

panels.title: Bytes Throughput – Outgoing Sockets
panels.description: rate() of bytes_acked_total / bytes_received_total for outgoing sockets only. Compare against the other direction's panel - a healthy link is not necessarily symmetric in absolute bytes, but a direction that flatlines to zero while the other is busy is a red flag.

targets.expr: rate(qfsd_portal_rpc_socket_segments_out_total{cluster=~"$cluster", node_id=~"$node_id", peer_cluster_uuid=~"$peer_cluster_uuid", remote_node=~"$remote_node", fs_id=~"$fs_id", type=~"$type", type="incoming"}[$__rate_interval])

rate(qfsd_portal_rpc_socket_segments_in_total{cluster=~"$cluster", node_id=~"$node_id", peer_cluster_uuid=~"$peer_cluster_uuid", remote_node=~"$remote_node", fs_id=~"$fs_id", type=~"$type", type="incoming"}[$__rate_interval])

panels.title: Segments Throughput – Incoming Sockets
panels.description: rate() of segments_out_total / segments_in_total for incoming sockets only. Useful alongside the bytes panel to distinguish "fewer, larger segments" from "fewer segments overall" when throughput drops.

targets.expr: rate(qfsd_portal_rpc_socket_segments_out_total{cluster=~"$cluster", node_id=~"$node_id", peer_cluster_uuid=~"$peer_cluster_uuid", remote_node=~"$remote_node", fs_id=~"$fs_id", type=~"$type", type="outgoing"}[$__rate_interval])

rate(qfsd_portal_rpc_socket_segments_in_total{cluster=~"$cluster", node_id=~"$node_id", peer_cluster_uuid=~"$peer_cluster_uuid", remote_node=~"$remote_node", fs_id=~"$fs_id", type=~"$type", type="outgoing"}[$__rate_interval])

panels.title: Segments Throughput – Outgoing Sockets
panels.description: rate() of segments_out_total / segments_in_total for outgoing sockets only. Useful alongside the bytes panel to distinguish "fewer, larger segments" from "fewer segments overall" when throughput drops.

panels.title: 6. Liveness & Staleness
panels.description: Time elapsed since this socket last sent data, received data, or received an ack. A stalled or dead link shows one or more of these climbing without bound instead of resetting near zero on every send/receive/ack. Split by direction for the same reason as the Throughput row: one direction can go silent while the other keeps talking.

targets.expr: qfsd_portal_rpc_socket_duration_since_data_sent_ms{cluster=~"$cluster", node_id=~"$node_id", peer_cluster_uuid=~"$peer_cluster_uuid", remote_node=~"$remote_node", fs_id=~"$fs_id", type=~"$type", type="incoming"}

qfsd_portal_rpc_socket_duration_since_data_received_ms{cluster=~"$cluster", node_id=~"$node_id", peer_cluster_uuid=~"$peer_cluster_uuid", remote_node=~"$remote_node", fs_id=~"$fs_id", type=~"$type", type="incoming"}

qfsd_portal_rpc_socket_duration_since_ack_received_ms{cluster=~"$cluster", node_id=~"$node_id", peer_cluster_uuid=~"$peer_cluster_uuid", remote_node=~"$remote_node", fs_id=~"$fs_id", type=~"$type", type="incoming"}

panels.title: Staleness – Incoming Sockets
panels.description: duration_since_data_sent_ms / duration_since_data_received_ms / duration_since_ack_received_ms for incoming sockets. Each should reset toward zero regularly; an unbounded climb on any of them means that particular activity (send/receive/ack) has stopped happening on this socket - i.e. the link, or this direction of it, is stalled or dead.

targets.expr: qfsd_portal_rpc_socket_duration_since_data_sent_ms{cluster=~"$cluster", node_id=~"$node_id", peer_cluster_uuid=~"$peer_cluster_uuid", remote_node=~"$remote_node", fs_id=~"$fs_id", type=~"$type", type="outgoing"}

qfsd_portal_rpc_socket_duration_since_data_received_ms{cluster=~"$cluster", node_id=~"$node_id", peer_cluster_uuid=~"$peer_cluster_uuid", remote_node=~"$remote_node", fs_id=~"$fs_id", type=~"$type", type="outgoing"}

qfsd_portal_rpc_socket_duration_since_ack_received_ms{cluster=~"$cluster", node_id=~"$node_id", peer_cluster_uuid=~"$peer_cluster_uuid", remote_node=~"$remote_node", fs_id=~"$fs_id", type=~"$type", type="outgoing"}

panels.title: Staleness – Outgoing Sockets
panels.description: duration_since_data_sent_ms / duration_since_data_received_ms / duration_since_ack_received_ms for outgoing sockets. Each should reset toward zero regularly; an unbounded climb on any of them means that particular activity (send/receive/ack) has stopped happening on this socket - i.e. the link, or this direction of it, is stalled or dead.

list.description: Local node reporting the socket. Narrowed by the selected Cluster(s).
list.label: Node
list.name: node_id

list.description: Far-side cluster this portal link connects to. Narrowed by the selected Cluster(s) and Node(s).
list.label: Peer Cluster
list.name: peer_cluster_uuid

list.description: Node ID on the peer cluster. Narrowed by the selected Cluster(s), Node(s), and Peer Cluster(s).
list.label: Peer Node
list.name: remote_node

list.description: Filesystem (portal replication session) ID multiplexed over the link. Narrowed by all variables to its left.
list.label: Filesystem
list.name: fs_id

list.description: Socket direction: "incoming" on the cluster that accepted the connection, "outgoing" on the cluster that initiated it. Narrowed by all variables to its left. Note the Throughput and Staleness rows below are always split into incoming/outgoing panels regardless of this filter - this variable additionally narrows those panels, and every other panel, to just the direction(s) selected.
list.label: Connection Type
list.name: type

title: Qumulo Portal RPC Socket - Cloud Data Fabric Link Health
